tasks.title: Write policy snapshot spec and operator docs

DR-0001: Update doctor outputs to include socket ownership/mode and group membership guidance.
Document that socket ACL is the authorization boundary for world-agent requests.
DR-0005: Add a deprecation spec that removes legacy local policy resolution in world-agent after a defined release window.
DR-0006:
Define the exact policy cache contract in the spec:
  - The shell must compute a cache key from: workspace root (or “no workspace”), global policy patch path, workspace policy patch path.
  - Before each executed command, the shell must `stat` the policy patch file(s) and invalidate the cache when metadata changes (mtime or size).
  - When `substrate policy global|workspace set|reset|init` runs in the same process, the shell must invalidate the cache after the write completes.
DR-0007: Update provisioning docs to treat the socket group as the access boundary and to document the implications on multi-user hosts.
Add an explicit “multi-user posture” section to the policy snapshot spec.
DR-0009: Update the snapshot spec to explicitly exclude `cmd_*` keys and state where they are enforced.
Add a manual testing section that demonstrates `cmd_denied` behavior is unchanged by the snapshot work.
DR-0010: Extend the snapshot spec to include the config refresh contract (global + workspace patch paths, cache keys, and invalidation rules).
DR-0011:
Add a platform parity section to the snapshot spec that states:
  - The host must attach a policy snapshot to world-agent requests on every platform where world-agent is used.
  - The world-agent must ignore local policy resolution for enforcement inputs when a valid snapshot is present.
  - When a snapshot is missing or invalid, the shell must record `policy_resolution_mode` in trace output and must fail closed if policy requires world execution.

outputs: Create `docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/policy-snapshot-spec.md` defining `PolicySnapshotV1`, enforcement boundary, cache contract, multi-user posture, and cross-platform parity contract.
Create `docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/policy-snapshot-deprecation-spec.md` defining legacy mode removal gates, release window, and compatibility rules.
Update existing operator docs that explain world-agent access boundary and multi-user implications (pick the canonical doc location and link it from the spec).

Add a manual testing section in `docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/policy-snapshot-spec.md` that demonstrates host `cmd_denied` enforcement remains active (exit code `126` in enforce mode).
acceptance_criteria: Spec defines a single, testable contract for snapshot contents, validation, and enforcement boundaries.
Spec defines the policy refresh contract and the config refresh contract with deterministic invalidation triggers that preserve next-command visibility.
Deprecation spec defines a single release window and a single removal gate for legacy local policy resolution in world-agent.
Docs name the authorization boundary as the world socket ACL and include operator guidance for socket ownership/mode and group membership.
tasks.kickoff_prompt:
Goal: write the policy snapshot spec and operator docs for the world-agent policy snapshot feature.

Read:
- docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/decision_register.md
- docs/project_management/next/ADR-0014-world-agent-policy-resolution-and-concurrency.md
- docs/project_management/standards/PLANNING_RESEARCH_AND_ALIGNMENT_STANDARD.md
- docs/project_management/next/ADR-0011-world-deps-packages-bundles-contract.md
- docs/WORLD.md
- docs/TRACE.md

Produce:
- docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/policy-snapshot-spec.md
- docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/policy-snapshot-deprecation-spec.md

Spec requirements:
- Define PolicySnapshotV1 fields and validation rules.
- Define enforcement boundary: host enforces cmd policy; world-agent enforces world_fs + net_allowed + limits.
- Define policy refresh contract and config refresh contract with deterministic invalidation that preserves next-command visibility after external file edits and in-process set/reset/init.
- Define multi-user posture and socket ACL boundary with explicit operator guidance.
- Define cross-platform parity contract (Linux/macOS/WSL) and explicit fallback rules.
- Define the legacy local policy resolution removal plan with a single release window and a single gating condition.

Output discipline:
- Use singular, testable behavior statements.
- Use exactly two options for any new decision recorded in a DR entry.

Update any in-repo `ExecuteRequest { ... }` constructors to include `policy_snapshot: None` until shell/world-agent plumbing is implemented.
Add serialization tests in `crates/agent-api-types/src/lib.rs` proving schema stability for `policy_snapshot` (absent vs present).
acceptance_criteria: `cargo test -p agent-api-types` passes.
JSON serialization round-trips for ExecuteRequest with and without `policy_snapshot`.
PolicySnapshotV1 is strict enough for versioning (explicit `schema_version: 1`).

tasks.title: Implement host policy snapshot resolver and attach snapshots to world-agent requests

DR-0002: Update shell routing to attach the snapshot to world-agent requests.
DR-0006: Add trace fields to record the policy snapshot hash so QA can detect policy drift across executions.
Add an integration test that edits a policy patch file and proves the change takes effect on the next command without restarting the shell.
DR-0010: Add an integration test that edits `config.yaml` and `workspace.yaml` and proves the next command reflects the new config without restarting the shell.
DR-0011: When a snapshot is missing or invalid, the shell must record `policy_resolution_mode` in trace output and must fail closed if policy requires world execution.

outputs: Add a host-side resolver that produces `PolicySnapshotV1` from the effective merged policy for the command cwd.
Attach `policy_snapshot` to `ExecuteRequest` in world-agent request paths (non-PTY + PTY) on every platform that uses world-agent transport.
Implement policy cache with deterministic invalidation via metadata change detection on global/workspace patch paths, preserving next-command visibility.
Implement config cache with the same invalidation contract as policy, preserving next-command visibility.
Record snapshot metadata in trace output: `policy_resolution_mode`, `policy_snapshot_schema`, `policy_snapshot_hash`.
acceptance_criteria: World-agent requests always include a snapshot payload when world execution is used and a snapshot schema is supported.
Policy cache invalidation triggers on file metadata changes and after in-process `policy global|workspace set|reset|init` writes complete.
Config cache invalidation triggers on file metadata changes and after in-process `config global|workspace set|reset|init` writes complete.
Trace output includes `policy_resolution_mode` and a stable `policy_snapshot_hash` for each executed command.

DR-0008: Extend `GET /v1/doctor/world` and `substrate world doctor --json` to report whether snapshot enforcement is supported and active.
DR-0011: The world-agent must ignore local policy resolution for enforcement inputs when a valid snapshot is present.

outputs: Replace per-request broker calls (`detect_profile`, `world_fs_policy`, `allowed_domains`) with snapshot-driven enforcement inputs when `policy_snapshot` is present and valid.
Define legacy local policy resolution behavior for missing snapshots during the deprecation window, and record `policy_resolution_mode` deterministically.
Extend world-agent doctor report to include snapshot capability and active mode information.
Update world-agent tests that construct `ExecuteRequest` to include `policy_snapshot` as required by the new schema.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent world-agent requests do not share policy state when snapshots are present.
World-agent behavior for world_fs and net allowlist is a deterministic function of snapshot inputs and agent-local canonicalization.
Doctor report includes snapshot capability and reports the active policy resolution mode.
`cargo test -p world-agent` passes.

tasks.kickoff_prompt:
Goal: run the CI cross-platform runners for the world-agent policy snapshot feature and record results.

Read:
- Makefile (targets: ci-testing, ci-compile-parity, feature-smoke, feature-smoke-all)

Preconditions:
- Run from the repo root.
- Use a non-main ref for workflow dispatch.

Commands:
- make ci-compile-parity CI_WORKFLOW_REF="$(git branch --show-current)" CI_REMOTE=origin
- make feature-smoke-all FEATURE_DIR="docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot" WORKFLOW_REF="$(git branch --show-current)" REMOTE=origin RUNNER_KIND=self-hosted

Artifact:
- Create or update docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md with: ref, date, dispatched targets, workflow run URLs, platforms executed, and PASS/FAIL summary.

tasks.title: Remediate CI failures with local repro and repeat-until-green loop

WAPS-0013: Both dispatched workflows failed; remediation must drive the next CI runs to PASS.

outputs: Fix all `ci-compile-parity` failures by reproducing locally with `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and applying targeted code changes until it passes cleanly.

Fix all `feature-smoke-all` failures by reproducing locally where possible (Linux smoke) and updating the feature smoke entrypoints to match the workflow contract (Windows script path), then rerunning cross-platform Feature Smoke until all platforms pass.
Update `docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md` with every rerun URL and final PASS/FAIL status per rerun.
acceptance_criteria: `make ci-compile-parity` reports PASS on `ubuntu-24.04`, `macos-14`, and `windows-2022` for the target ref.
`make feature-smoke-all FEATURE_DIR=docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot` reports PASS on `linux`, `macos`, and `windows` for the target ref.
`docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md` records all reruns and ends with a PASS entry for both workflows.
tasks.kickoff_prompt:
Goal: remediate CI failures with an explicit repeat-until-green loop: fix -> local repro -> CI cross-platform rerun -> repeat.

Inputs:
- Last failing runs are recorded in docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md.

Step 1: fetch exact CI failure messages
- gh run view <RUN_ID> --repo atomize-hq/substrate --log-failed | sed -n '1,220p'

Step 2: reproduce locally (compile-parity gate)
- cargo fmt --all -- --check
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test --workspace --all-targets

Step 3: reproduce locally (feature smoke where possible)
- cargo build -p substrate
- PATH="$(pwd)/target/debug:$PATH" bash docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/smoke/linux-smoke.sh

Step 4: apply targeted fixes
- Fix code or scripts that caused failures.
- Re-run Step 2 and Step 3 until both are clean locally.

Step 5: dispatch CI cross-platform runners
- make ci-compile-parity CI_WORKFLOW_REF="$(git branch --show-current)" CI_REMOTE=origin
- make feature-smoke-all FEATURE_DIR="docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot" WORKFLOW_REF="$(git branch --show-current)" REMOTE=origin RUNNER_KIND=self-hosted

Step 6: record CI reruns
- Append run URLs, platforms, and PASS/FAIL to docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md.

Loop condition:
- Repeat Steps 1–6 until both workflows report PASS across all platforms.

tasks.title: Remove Windows smoke skipping and make WSL world execution reliable
covers_misalignment: WAPS-0009 acceptance: Windows smoke must validate executable world-agent behavior, not only doctor reachability.
WAPS-0011 acceptance: when doctor reports snapshot support, smoke must assert per-test trace metadata instead of emitting skipped=true.
outputs: Eliminate the Windows smoke early-exit skip gate in `docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/smoke/windows-wsl-smoke.ps1` and replace it with a deterministic WSL world-exec preflight that either runs the full smoke suite or fails with a high-signal diagnostic.

Fix the root cause of WSL world execution hangs/timeouts on the Windows self-hosted runner by applying targeted changes to the Windows WSL backend transport and runner preflight scripts so `substrate --world --ci --command "true __waps_preflight__<run_id>__"` completes consistently.
Update `docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md` with the CI rerun URL where Windows Feature Smoke passes without skipped execution.
acceptance_criteria: Windows Feature Smoke produces a JSON summary where `skipped=false` and at least one test contains non-null trace metadata with `policy_resolution_mode == snapshot_v1`.
`make feature-smoke-all FEATURE_DIR=docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot` reports PASS for `windows` with the WSL smoke suite executing (no skip gate) on the target ref.
At least one root-cause fix is committed (backend/transport/preflight) that reduces or eliminates the observed WSL world-exec timeouts on the Windows self-hosted runner.
tasks.kickoff_prompt:
Goal: make Windows Feature Smoke execute WSL world runs reliably (no skipped=true) and pass cross-platform CI.

Read:
- docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md
- docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/smoke/windows-smoke.ps1
- docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/smoke/windows-wsl-smoke.ps1
- scripts/windows/wsl-warm.ps1
- crates/world-windows-wsl/src/backend.rs
- .github/workflows/feature-smoke.yml

Constraints:
- The smoke script must not return PASS by skipping world execution.
- The smoke script must fail with actionable diagnostics when world execution is unavailable.

Work:
1) Remove the `doctor_ok` early-exit skip gate and replace it with a deterministic preflight that runs a world command using the existing run id marker:
   - Command: `true __waps_preflight__${runId}__`
   - Timeout: strict and short
   - Capture stdout/stderr to log files and include them in the failure output
2) Reproduce the hang/timeout via local Windows + WSL runs or via the self-hosted runner logs, then fix the root cause by making the WSL backend transport fail-fast with timeouts and clear errors instead of hanging.
3) Ensure the smoke run emits per-test trace metadata including `policy_resolution_mode`, `policy_snapshot_schema`, and `policy_snapshot_hash`.

Validate locally:
- pwsh -File docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/smoke/windows-smoke.ps1

Dispatch CI and record results:
- make feature-smoke-all FEATURE_DIR="docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot" WORKFLOW_REF="$(git branch --show-current)" REMOTE=origin RUNNER_KIND=self-hosted
- Update docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md with the PASS run URL and note that Windows ran without skipped execution.

tasks.title: Strengthen Windows smoke parity checks (write allowlist + trace invariants)
covers_misalignment: WAPS-0009 acceptance: smoke must assert filesystem allowlist behavior and trace invariants on every required platform.
outputs: Extend `docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/smoke/windows-wsl-smoke.ps1` to execute concrete filesystem allowlist checks in full isolation writable mode (allowlisted write succeeds; non-allowlisted write fails with read-only filesystem semantics) and to record these outcomes in the JSON summary.

Add strict assertions that `policy_resolution_mode == snapshot_v1`, `policy_snapshot_schema == 1`, and `policy_snapshot_hash` is non-empty for every executed test when doctor reports snapshot support.
Update `docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md` with the CI rerun URL where Windows smoke passes with allowlist checks enabled.
acceptance_criteria: Windows smoke runs a write-allowlisted `touch` in `./writable/` that succeeds and a non-allowlisted `touch` outside that prefix that fails with a read-only filesystem error, and these results are reflected in the summary JSON.
Windows smoke fails if any executed test lacks `policy_snapshot_hash` or reports `policy_resolution_mode != snapshot_v1` when snapshot support is available.
`make feature-smoke-all FEATURE_DIR=docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot` reports PASS for `windows` with the allowlist checks enabled.
tasks.kickoff_prompt:
Goal: strengthen Windows WAPS smoke validation to cover filesystem allowlist behavior and trace invariants.

Read:
- docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/policy-snapshot-spec.md
- docs/TRACE.md
- docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/smoke/windows-wsl-smoke.ps1
- .github/workflows/feature-smoke.yml

Work:
1) Update the Windows WSL smoke to run concrete file write checks for full isolation writable:
   - Create `./writable/` under the temp workspace.
   - Run a world command that `touch`es `./writable/allowed.txt` and assert success.
   - Run a world command that `touch`es `./not-allowlisted/disallowed.txt` and assert failure with a read-only filesystem error.
2) Ensure per-test trace metadata extraction captures and asserts:
   - policy_resolution_mode == snapshot_v1
   - policy_snapshot_schema == 1
   - policy_snapshot_hash is present and non-empty

Validate locally:
- pwsh -File docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/smoke/windows-smoke.ps1

Dispatch CI and record results:
- make feature-smoke-all FEATURE_DIR="docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot" WORKFLOW_REF="$(git branch --show-current)" REMOTE=origin RUNNER_KIND=self-hosted
- Update docs/project_management/_archived/world-agent-policy-snapshot/ci_runs.md with the PASS run URL and note that Windows ran allowlist write assertions.
